final_rules.name: require_header
final_rules.description: Every table must have at least one non-empty header row (thead or first row with th elements).
final_rules.condition: num_header_rows == 0 or all header cells are empty

final_rules.name: column_count_consistency
final_rules.description: At least 65% of rows must share the same column count. Rows with colspan are allowed to differ.

final_rules.name: empty_cell_ratio
final_rules.description: Tables with more than 55% empty cells are likely parsing collapses, not sparse data tables.

final_rules.name: single_col_collapse
final_rules.description: If more than 25% of body rows have exactly 1 cell, the table likely collapsed during extraction.

final_rules.name: span_sanity
final_rules.description: Any cell with colspan > 15 or rowspan > 20 indicates a corrupted span attribute from extraction.

final_rules.name: duplicate_row_detection
final_rules.description: More than 20% identical body rows signals a repeated-extraction bug, not real data repetition.

final_rules.name: ocr_character_confusion
final_rules.description: Detects l/1 and O/0 OCR substitutions common in scanned medical PDFs. More than 5% affected cells = untrusted numeric content.

final_rules.name: numeric_garble
final_rules.description: Digits with embedded non-numeric characters (e.g. 12abc34) indicate OCR garbling of numbers, critical in medical tables.

final_rules.name: whitespace_dominance
final_rules.description: Cells containing only whitespace, dashes, or dots (layout fill characters) exceeding 45% of non-empty cells.

final_rules.name: cell_length_outlier
final_rules.description: Any cell longer than 800 characters likely contains merged paragraph text, not a table cell value.

final_rules.name: numeric_garble_strict
final_rules.description: Stricter secondary rule for 'numeric_garble' learned from PubTabNet FN analysis. 25th-percentile of missed values was 0.100, well below primary threshold 0.18.
